target.title: Exact-review capacity queue times out before issue/PR reviews run
target.body:
## Summary

Exact issue/PR review runs are currently capped by the exact-review capacity semaphore, but overflow work waits inside already-started GitHub Actions runs and then fails after the 20 minute capacity timeout. Under burst load this leaves issues/PRs without a ClawSweeper review comment or labels.

This is separate from #304. PR #304 addresses the case where Codex already produced a decision but `decision.rootCauseCluster` made the decision schema-invalid. This issue covers runs that never reach the review/comment/label stage because they time out or stall while waiting for exact-review capacity.

## Evidence

Current main has an exact-review cap:

- `config/automation-limits.json` sets `lanes.exact_review.max_concurrent` to `4`.
- `.github/workflows/sweep.yml` runs `pnpm run repair:codex-capacity` with `--timeout-ms 1200000`.
- `src/repair/codex-capacity.ts` admits only runs whose rank is inside the configured exact-review limit.

Live behavior observed on June 17, 2026:

- 75 active exact-review runs were present.
- Recent `Review event item ...` failures lasted around 20-22 minutes.
- Example: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27673696915 for `openclaw/openclaw#94004` failed at the capacity step with:

```text
timed out waiting for Codex capacity: exact rank 6/37, 4 exact-review slots configured
```

A sampled admitted run waited about 17 minutes at `Wait for exact-review Codex capacity`, then reached Codex and completed review. Later-ranked runs remained stuck at the capacity step.

Additional live evidence from the re-review of #311:

- Run https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/runs/27678181750/job/81858579747 entered `Wait for exact-review Codex capacity`.
- The capacity helper repeatedly logged normal rank updates, then hit GitHub API installation rate limiting while polling Actions state:

```text
Could not verify Codex capacity: Command failed: gh api --method GET repos/openclaw/clawsweeper/actions/workflows/sweep.yml/runs?per_page=100&event=repository_dispatch&status=in_progress&page=1 --jq .workflow_runs
API rate limit exceeded for installation ... (HTTP 403)
```

- A live snapshot at the same time showed this run ranked 4 of 53 exact-review waiters with an exact limit of 4. In other words, it should have been admissible, but the semaphore could not reliably verify capacity because its polling hit the GitHub App installation rate limit.

That points to a second failure mode inside the same capacity gate: many waiting runs can create a polling stampede against the Actions API. Once the app installation is rate-limited, even an otherwise-admissible run can remain stuck in the capacity wait step.

## Impact

When the queue is deeper than the 20 minute wait window, ClawSweeper fails before it can publish a review comment or apply advisory labels. From the target repository this looks like ClawSweeper never ran.

The API-rate-limit behavior makes the queue less reliable under exactly the conditions where it is needed most: high burst load. It can prevent slot admission even when a run is within the configured `4` exact-review slots.

## Expected behavior

Capacity overflow should not be terminal or lossy. One of these should happen:

- exact-review work is durably queued before a worker run starts;
- capacity timeouts automatically requeue/backfill the same item;
- the timeout is treated as a retryable scheduler condition rather than a failed review;
- the exact-review lane has enough admission/backpressure to avoid accumulating doomed waiters; or
- capacity admission uses a shared/low-rate signal instead of every waiting run polling the Actions API independently.

The fix should include visible telemetry so maintainers can distinguish `waiting for capacity`, `reviewing`, `timed out but requeued`, `rate-limited while waiting`, and `permanently failed`.
